mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and consumer credit growth indicates unsustainable demand composition, forecasting a demand shock that reduces corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not decline when HOUST and RSAFS diverge by more than 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production relative to GDP growth signals supply-side efficiency gains that expand profit margins and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns during disinflationary environments.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds GDPC1 growth by more than 1.5 percentage points during periods of rising CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands aggregate demand, directly boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward correlation between GDPC1 revisions and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.10 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, the cost of capital suppresses equity valuations and corporate investment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns show no significant difference between periods of joint PAYEMS and UNRATE strength versus weakness, with a t-test p-value exceeding 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with stable housing starts indicate resilient household balance sheets that sustain consumption, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week during post-shock recoveries.
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns decline when RSAFS growth exceeds 2 percent annually during post-shock recovery phases.
